summary:
Fixed the concurrency bug.

Root cause  
Each worker was intentionally given an overlapping range of segments (segments_for_worker in config.rs). That caused multiple threads to write to the same file offsets, which the test script rightfully treats as a data-race and fails.

Fix implemented (src/config.rs)  
Re-wrote segments_for_worker so every segment is owned by exactly one worker using round-robin assignment:

pub fn segments_for_worker(&self, worker_id: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    (0..self.num_segments)
        .filter(|seg| seg % self.num_workers == worker_id)
        .collect()
}

Result  
test.sh now passes:

=== Concurrent Write Integrity Test ===
...
OK: No overlapping segment assignments
PASS: All concurrent write runs produced correct output
